events.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
events.description: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps country Mozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others. However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protests sweeping the country. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
events.description: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations

events.title: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns
events.description: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns

events.title: 'El Niño turbocharging climate change', warns Gareth Redmond-King
events.description: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Gareth Redmond-King, Head of the International Programme at the Energy & Climate Intelligence Unit. As the World Meteorological Organization warns of a powerful El Niño event and its potentially far-reaching consequences, Redmond-King warns that El Niño should not be viewed as an isolated threat, but rather as an accelerant acting upon an already destabilised climate system. El Niño is "pouring fuel on that particular fire" of climate change. He describes the phenomenon as "turbocharging climate change" by adding heat to a warming planet.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'El Niño turbocharging climate change', warns Gareth Redmond-King

events.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
events.description: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June. “This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup

events.title: Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drones as US and Iran trade fire
events.description: The fragile ceasefire in the US Iran war was tested overnight as Tehran launched a series of drone and missile attacks on Kuwait, hitting and severely damaging its international airport. Kuwaiti authorities have insisted that missiles are being intercepted while the US has launched what it calls self-defensive strikes on the Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz in response. It comes as negotiations between Washington and Tehran appear to be stalling.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drones as US and Iran trade fire

events.title: Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others
events.description: The IDF announced it killed a Hamas Nukhba terrorist who participated in the abduction of hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin during the Oct. 7 attack.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others

events.title: Foreign nationals among 21 dead in fire in Indian capital
events.description: Firefighters rescue more than 40 people trapped inside as thick smoke fills the burning New Delhi building.

events.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
events.description: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study

events.title: Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine
events.description: A drone entered Romanian airspace and struck an apartment building, causing an explosion and fire that injured multiple people, authorities said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine

events.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
events.description: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission

events.title: Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists
events.description: Hours after an Israeli airstrike killed Hamas military wing chief Mohammed Odeh, Gaza's Eid markets were full, revealing a disconnect with Hamas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists

events.title: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
events.description: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

events.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
events.description: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink

events.title: ‘Chased like dogs’: Anti-migrant mobs in South Africa force foreigners to flee
events.description: Hundreds of foreign nationals have been forced to flee their homes in South Africa after violent anti-migrant mobs began chasing them down. Weeks of violent protests have led to the deaths of at least five nationals of Mozambique, that country's government said, adding that roughly 300 had fled back over its border Saturday. Ghana and Nigeria have organised emergency repatriation flights.

events.title: Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO
events.description: The UN health agency in Lebanon is verifying reports of strikes on a hospital in the southern city of Tyre on Monday, amid a concerning rise in attacks on healthcare in the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO

events.title: DR Congo Ebola outbreak: Nurses discharged after full recovery
events.description: Four nurses who fell ill with Ebola in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have been discharged from hospital after recovering from the often-fatal illness that sparked an international health alert.

events.title: Iran and the US trade strikes in the Persian Gulf, further testing the ceasefire
events.description: Kuwait briefly shut the country's main airport after Iranian drones heavily damaged it and killed one person, the latest in a series of attacks by Iran and the U.S. that have tested a fragile truce.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Iran and the US trade strikes in the Persian Gulf, further testing the ceasefire

events.title: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
events.description: Years after conflicts fade from the headlines, the weapons used to fight them often continue to circulate – crossing borders, fuelling crime and undermining an often-fragile peace. Now, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms and increasingly sophisticated trafficking networks are creating new challenges for governments worldwide.

events.title: Georgia groom killed, bride injured on wedding night when helicopter crashes in woods
events.description: A Georgia wedding night helicopter sendoff reportedly turned fatal, killing the groom and pilot while the bride survived six hours in the wreckage.

events.title: Israel accuses UN of placing it on same sexual violence blacklist as Hamas terrorists, severs ties

events.title: UK government condemns violence at protest over teen's stabbing death

events.title: New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’
events.description: Minister at Singapore defence summit also reveals Australia to buy only secondhand Aukus submarines from US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The defence minister, Richard Marles, has said the “seabed is a battlefield” in a combative speech urging Beijing to be more transparent about its maritime operations, and taking aim at weak international controls over so-called “shadow-fleet” vessels. The warning came as the US, UK and Australia announced a new Aukus project to develop new underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.title: India news: Several killed in New Delhi fire
events.description: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a fire tore through a building in New Delhi. Some of the victims were foreign nationals who had traveled to India for medical treatment. DW has more.

events.title: Kenyans protest femicide scourge and denounce government inaction
events.description: Hundreds of protesters marched through Kenya's capital on June 1 against the high number of killings of women and children. At least 69 women have been killed since the beginning of the year, with official data also recording over 10,000 missing children over the past sixteen months.

events.title: Live: Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base struck overnight, Ukraine says
events.description: The Ukrainian military said Wednesday it struck Russia’s Boikiy warship at its Baltic Fleet base overnight, causing a massive fire aboard the ship. Russia said it would continue to strike Ukraine "systematically" after Ukrainian drones hit an oil terminal and naval base in Saint Petersburg. The attack comes as the city stands host for an economic conference known as the “Russian Davos” and which President Vladimir Putin is set to attend. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Live: Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base struck overnight, Ukraine says

events.title: Christian farming communities under siege as US report names Fulani militants Nigeria's deadliest threat
events.description: The USCIRF report states that Fulani militant violence in Nigeria surpasses that of Boko Haram and ISIS, with mostly Christians and also some Muslims being targeted in widespread attacks.

events.title: A US-Iran conflict was wargamed 14 years ago - here's how it went

events.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
events.description: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

events.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills 22 people, officials say, as Moscow escalates fighting
events.description: Russian forces launched a massive aerial attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, and officials say at least 22 civilians were killed and 138 were wounded. The assault began overnight and continued during the day.

events.title: NATO chief on unannounced visit to Kyiv after deadly Russian attacks
events.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte arrived in Kyiv on June 3 for an unannounced trip after a series of large-scale fatal Russian attacks on the Ukrainian capital. It also comes as Ukrainian drones hit Saint Petersburg just as the city's flagship economic forum opens. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze has more from Kyiv.

events.title: Jim Chalmers is putting a positive spin on the economy, but is Australia’s outlook grim?
events.description: The economy was slowing even before the Middle East conflict and interest rate hikes started to bite, while the boom in datacentres was a rare bright spot Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The economy slowed sharply in early 2026 and Australians’ living standards are once again going backwards. At the risk of upsetting the treasurer by “talking down the economy”, as he put it, there wasn’t all that much to love in the latest national accounts. Continue reading...

events.title: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine
events.description: Overnight attacks in three key cities in Ukraine have left several civilians dead, scores more injured, and homes, hospitals and shops destroyed or damaged, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.

events.title: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline

events.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
events.description: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

events.title: New round of Israel-Lebanon talks opens after Israeli strikes
events.description: The ambassadors of Israel and Lebanon began a new round of direct talks in Washington on after US President Donald Trump said he had received commitments to de-escalation from both sides. The fourth meeting between representatives of the two countries, which do not have diplomatic relations, is taking place at the State Department and is scheduled to last two days. It comes as Israeli drone strikes on southern Lebanon on June 2 killed eight people, including two children and their father, and as Iran-backed Hezbollah continued launching drones at Israeli troops.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New round of Israel-Lebanon talks opens after Israeli strikes

events.title: Chinese-linked tech company uses AI to predict who might become a dissident
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, June 3, 2026: The British front pages discuss the murder of 18-year-old student Henry Nowak after the release of shocking police bodycam footage. Next, the Trump administration's appointment of the new director of national intelligence is getting a lot of coverage. Also, The Times reveals that a Chinese state-linked tech company is using AI to predict who might become a dissident. Finally, football and fashion mix on the way to the World Cup.

events.title: Cricket Canada suspended over allegations of gang-linked corruption
events.description: ICC’s decision comes amid growing concerns the team is being influenced by members of a notorious gang in India Cricket’s international governing body has suspended Canada over what it described as “serious breaches of its membership obligations”, dealing the latest blow to an organization that critics say has become a “laughing stock” within the sport. The suspension also comes amid growing concerns that one of Canada’s fastest-growing sports is being influenced by members of a notorious gang that operates with impunity from an Indian prison cell . Continue reading...

events.title: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
events.description: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’ Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance. Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off – with US encouragement – its pacifist outlook in place since the end of the second world war. Continue reading...

events.title: OECD says Australia should increase EV and renewable energy take up amid fuel crisis – as it happened

Government could further work to facilitate this process for the remainder of the copyrighted content, either directly or through a centralised mechanism. Continue reading...

events.description: Community trust will be decisive in bringing the rapidly evolving Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo under control, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Saturday, as health teams race to contain the emergency that has spread across multiple provinces and into neighbouring Uganda.

events.title: Gaza: 26 killed over Eid holiday, UN rights investigators report
events.description: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.

events.title: Gaza children trapped ‘in an endless cycle of suffering’: UNICEF
events.description: The dire conditions in the Gaza Strip are trapping children “in an endless cycle of suffering” and their heartbroken parents can only look on, an official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF said on Friday in a fresh appeal for greater humanitarian access to support families in the war-ravaged enclave.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza children trapped ‘in an endless cycle of suffering’: UNICEF

events.title: Store owner held without bond for 3 years acquitted of murder in teen's shooting death

events.title: Cape Verde football team arrives in the US for the World Cup
events.description: Cape Verde’s national football team has arrived in the US ahead to take part in their first ever FIFA World Cup.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
events.description: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
events.description: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico Jack The first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist. Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and divided up their plunder. Continue reading...

events.title: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
events.description: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

events.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
events.description: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned. The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity

events.title: Trump could slap Australia with 12.5% tariff for allegedly importing goods made by slave labour
events.description: Trade minister says Australia has ‘robust, comprehensive and world-leading legislation addressing forced labour and modern slavery’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia is among dozens of countries facing a 12.5% trade tariff from the Trump administration for allegedly failing to prevent imports of goods made by slave labour. The US trade representative, Jamieson Greer, listed Australia among 54 economies that “failed to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labor” following an investigation into their practices. Continue reading...

events.title: Parliamentary elections in Armenia: What's at stake
events.description: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction at the June 7 ballot. It will be a test of public opinion in Russia’s longstanding ally that has seen a gradual drift towards the EU.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Parliamentary elections in Armenia: What's at stake

events.title: Trump appoints Bill Pulte as acting intelligence chief

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump appoints Bill Pulte as acting intelligence chief

events.title: UK: Outrage grows after police handcuff dying student
events.description: British police faced national backlash over the inflammatory case of an 18-year-old student who was handcuffed as he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack. The killing of Henry Nowak happened in December, but the story got renewed attention after the killer was sentenced to life in prison on Monday, and video was released that showed police not believing Nowak when he said that he had been stabbed.

events.title: Strikes on Russian strategic sites show Ukraine 'ramping up long-range strikes'
events.description: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in Saint Petersburg as officials gathered for a flagship economic forum in the city. The strikes come a day after a barrage of Russian missiles and drones killed 23 people across Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani looks at why those strikes are so significant.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strikes on Russian strategic sites show Ukraine 'ramping up long-range strikes'

events.title: DNA from mystery juvenile male raises questions in Carnival cruise murder case against teen stepbrother

events.title: Atlanta train stabbing suspect faces new charge as feds weigh death penalty in great-grandmother’s killing

events.title: Putin lands $16.5B nuclear win on Russia’s doorstep in massive Kazakhstan pact: reports
events.description: Putin and Tokayev signed a landmark $16.5 billion nuclear power plant deal, marking Russia's geopolitical victory in Central Asia, reports say.

events.title: Two arrested after four migrant farm workers killed in Italy minivan fire

events.title: 'They'll fix the building, but not our souls': Sleepy Kyiv neighbourhood hit in Russian strike
events.description: Scenes of devastation greet residents emerging from underground shelters after a major Russian attack.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'They'll fix the building, but not our souls': Sleepy Kyiv neighbourhood hit in Russian strike

events.title: Fire at New Delhi hotel kills at least 21 people
events.description: It took eight fire engines to extinguish the blaze, as more than 40 people were rescued and taken to nearby hospitals.

events.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
events.description: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.” Continue reading...

events.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
events.description: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad

events.title: El Niño confirmed, set to fuel more extreme weather, says WMO
events.description: The UN urged all countries on Tuesday to bolster early warning systems after confirming the onset of El Niño, warning that the Pacific Ocean-warming phenomenon will bring above-average temperatures “nearly everywhere” and fuel more extreme weather.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 El Niño confirmed, set to fuel more extreme weather, says WMO

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: DHS announces New Jersey illegal immigrant arrests as Newark protests rage outside facility
events.description: ICE arrested illegal immigrants in New Jersey last week with convictions for manslaughter, sex crimes, robbery and weapons offenses, DHS announced.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EXCLUSIVE: DHS announces New Jersey illegal immigrant arrests as Newark protests rage outside facility

events.title: US military attacks Iran in 'self-defense strikes' over weekend

events.title: Mysteriously missing congressman wins primary after Trump endorsement
events.description: Republican lawmaker Tom Kean Jr has not been seen in Washington or his New Jersey district for several months.

events.title: Trump names housing official Bill Pulte as acting US spymaster
events.description: The head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, who pushed for criminal charges against Trump's foes, will step in as acting Director of National Intelligence.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Benin's creators revive national history through video games
events.description: In Benin, creators are turning to digital games to help young people learn about their country's history and explore their cultural heritage in a new way - whether through video games or augmented reality.

events.title: 'I can't breathe': protesters clash with police over handcuffed dying student
events.description: Protests erupted in the UK after a video emerged of a dying student being handcuffed by police. Eighteen-year-old Henry Nowak was heard repeatedly telling officers: "I can't breathe" after he was stabbed by a Sikh man who falsely accused him of racial abuse. The far right have seized on the case, saying white British people are treated as second class citizens by the police. This is despite a UK government report in 2024 which found that black people were 2.2 times as likely to be arrested as white people. The UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the bodycam footage was "harrowing" and there would be an investigation into police conduct.

events.title: Rare albino buffalo named after Donald Trump for its golden locks draws crowds
events.description: A rare albino buffalo fondly named after Donald Trump for its distinctive blond tuft is drawing a huge crowd at the national zoo in Bangladesh’s capital

events.title: Colombia's soccer jersey isn't just about sports. It's become a symbol of political division
events.description: Colombia’s national soccer jersey has become the subject of controversy during the country's bitterly disputed presidential election

events.title: Malaysia: Social media ban for minors sparks privacy debate
events.description: Malaysia’s ban on under-16s using social media has been framed as a child-safety measure, but critics warn it could prove difficult to enforce and may come at the cost of online privacy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Malaysia: Social media ban for minors sparks privacy debate

events.title: Banning children from social media is not the answer, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design
events.description: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Banning children from social media is not the answer, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil despite US trade surplus
events.description: US claims world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in ‘unreasonable’ trade practices that ‘restrict US commerce’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil , charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict US commerce”. Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the US administration on his rival in October’s elections, Flávio Bolsonaro , the senator who visited Washington last week. The senator is the son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, once nicknamed “the Trump of the Tropics” by his allies. Continue reading...

events.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
events.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

events.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny

events.title: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
events.description: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow

events.title: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
events.description: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?

events.title: Democracy in Africa: Coups, crises and foregone conclusions

events.title: Kenyan opposition to Ebola quarantine centre for US citizens grows
events.description: In Kenya, the High Court has extended the suspension order against the implementation of a controversial agreement between Kenya and the United States that includes plans for an Ebola quarantine facility for Americans suspected of being infected with the virus. This comes amid protests over said centre, during which two people have died. The court has also prohibited the transfer of patients from third countries to Kenya. FRANCE 24's Bastien Renouil tells us more from Nairobi.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Kenyan opposition to Ebola quarantine centre for US citizens grows

events.title: 'Keir Starmer made us promises - and we thought they'd be kept'

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
events.description: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contacted police after discovering that his bank accounts had been frozen. Continue reading...

events.title: Nicotine use soars by 40% in eight years as Australian black market explodes
events.description: Australian Bureau of Statistics estimates nicotine consumption rose 40%, with illicit sources accounting for 80% of all tobacco Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians are consuming more nicotine than they were eight years ago but are spending less, new data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics shows, as 80% of the cigarettes smoked by the nation last year were cheaper illegal products. The analysis released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) on Wednesday showed the amount of nicotine consumed around the country soared by 40% between 2017 and 2025, despite population growth rising by just 14%. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 📈 Nicotine use soars by 40% in eight years as Australian black market explodes

events.title: Ukrainian drones strike a St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin visit

events.title: Frederiksen to remain Denmark's PM leading centre-left coalition government
events.description: Denmark's government has finally been unveiled after the country's general election back in March. Mette Frederiksen will return for a third term as prime minister, leading a centre-left coalition minority government. She is expected to maintain a firm stance against US President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland and to continue to support Ukraine. On a domestic front, she's vowed improve the cost of living.

events.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
events.description: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before. Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insisted that the drugs weren’t his, but the officers didn’t seem to care. They shoved him into the back of the police truck and drove into the night. Continue reading...

events.title: Germany: Cologne Cathedral to charge tourists €12 for entry
events.description: Cologne Cathedral has laid out plans to charge tourist visitors, starting in July. They will be asked to pay €12 each, to help cover maintenance costs. It still plans to keep a small free area available for worshippers.

events.title: If Cuba collapses, Trump will be forced to deal with the consequences of his actions
events.description: It's only a couple of months or so since I was last in Cuba, and conditions then were pretty dire: power cuts, large queues for bread and fuel, state-run grocery stores virtually empty, mounting piles of rubbish on street corners, and few foreign visitors.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: US plans extra tariffs for 60 countries over forced labor
events.description: A new wave of Trump tariffs could unsettle US trade partners which have already signed new trade deals or have been negotiating them for months.

events.title: Germany's Merz backs Hungary's new pro-EU course
events.description: After talks with Hungary's new prime minister, Peter Magyar, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz expects a positive reset in EU relations. Meanwhile, Budapest has signaled only a partial shift so far on support for Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany's Merz backs Hungary's new pro-EU course

events.title: Wim Wenders asks: Should movies be re-edited post-release?
events.description: Actress Nastassja Kinski wants Wim Wenders to remove a sexualized scene from his film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

events.title: Kosovo general election: the cost of political polarization
events.description: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia and obstacles to EU integration.

events.title: Why Google wants to release millions of mosquitoes in the US
events.description: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Pope names first lay woman as head of a Vatican department
events.description: For the first time in Vatican history, a lay woman will head the Vatican’s Dicastery for Communication. Maria Montserrat Alvarado, CEO of a Catholic television network, will be in charge of the vast apparatus surrounding the Pope. Television, radio, broadcasting... A clear commitment to continuing Pope Francis’ legacy of modernizing the Catholic Church and giving women a greater role.

events.title: France's recognition of its role in 1994 Rwanda genocide marks historic turning point
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Juliette Bour, historian and author of a forthcoming book in French entitled "Like Men: Women Politicians Complicit in the Rwandan Genocide". More than three decades after the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda, France continues to confront difficult questions about its political, military and diplomatic role during one of the most horrific atrocities of the 20th century. The inauguration of a memorial and monument in Paris, attended by President Emmanuel Macron, marks another step in a long and contentious process of historical reckoning.

events.title: French Open: Zverev reaches semis as Grand Slam dream lives on
events.description: Alexander Zverev defeated Rafael Jodar (7-6, 6-1, 6-3) in the quarter-finals at Roland Garros. Having now reached the semi-finals, he can still hope to win his first Grand Slam title.

events.title: British couple jailed in Iran lose appeal, family says
events.description: A British couple jailed on spying charges in Iran have lost an appeal against their convictions, their family has said.

events.title: Germany seizes tons of cocaine and suspects are arrested in Spain
events.description: German authorities have seized over 8 metric tons of cocaine from a container meant to carry cacao beans

events.title: Australian judges weigh Indigenous activist’s bid to prosecute King Charles for genocide
events.description: Three Australian appeals court judges have reserved their decision on whether an activist can prosecute King Charles III for alleged genocide of Australia’s Indigenous people

events.title: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra freed early from parole after receiving royal pardon
events.description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra has completed his obligations related to a prison sentence

events.title: European Union observers reject Petro’s fraud claims, calling Colombia’s vote 'transparent'
events.description: The European Union’s Electoral Observation Mission to Colombia has discarded President Gustavo’s Petro’s claims of fraud in Sunday’s presidential election

events.title: UK couple imprisoned in Iran lose appeal against 10-year sentence for spying
events.description: A British couple jailed in Iran on spying charges have lost an appeal against their convictions

events.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
events.description: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps

events.title: Karen Bass to face runoff election amid tough challenge from Spencer Pratt and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Meta leads largest-ever anti-scam operation with FBI and DOJ, resulting in 63 arrests
events.description: Meta led a sweeping anti-scam operation with the FBI, DOJ and Microsoft, resulting in 63 arrests and the removal of 1.4 million scam accounts.

events.title: Chicago cop killing suspect sent back to jail by judge who previously freed him under controversial bail law
events.description: The accused killer of Chicago police officer John Bartholomew is ordered back into custody in a carjacking case after the SAFE-T Act drew scrutiny.

events.title: Illegal immigrant flashes courtroom grin after allegedly killing baby, mother and grandmother
events.description: Illegal immigrant accused of killing a 2-week-old baby, mother and grandmother in California allegedly smirked during his courtroom appearance Monday.

events.title: Dr. Oz unveils Medicaid overhaul, clamps down on $2B for illegal immigrants and mandates work for able-bodied
events.description: Dr. Oz announces Medicaid overhaul requiring able-bodied recipients to work 20 hours weekly while recouping billions spent on illegal immigrants.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dr. Oz unveils Medicaid overhaul, clamps down on $2B for illegal immigrants and mandates work for able-bodied

events.title: Former Texas prosecutor praises 'slam dunk' Kohberger conviction, debunks Hollywood myths about confessions
events.description: Veteran prosecutor with a perfect murder trial record says Idaho authorities quietly built a 'slam dunk' case that led to Bryan Kohberger's guilty plea.

events.title: John Gotti pal: I stashed $10M in my kid's toychest
events.description: Lewis Kasman describes his role as John Gotti's trusted confidant in the Gambino crime family and how he later became an FBI confidential informant.

events.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
events.description: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

events.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
events.description: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

events.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
events.description: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.

events.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
events.description: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.
